title: A YouTuber just recreated the original Star Wars with cardboard, and it's awesome (video)
summary: A YouTuber recreated the original Star Wars using cardboard, showcasing creativity and fandom. Zach King, known for his visual effects, brought the 1977 film to life in a unique way.

title: 13 International 'Star Wars' Posters You've Likely Never Seen
summary: A collection of 13 unique 'Star Wars' posters from various countries, including Japan and Turkey, is being celebrated for Star Wars Day. The posters feature original artwork inspired by the classic trilogy.

title: Why were galaxies so active in the early universe? We may be getting close to the answer
summary: Early galaxies were highly active in star formation, consuming gas and producing stars rapidly. A new model provides insights into why these galaxies were so different in the early universe.

title: 'It was quite a light show!' NASA astronaut spies dramatic fireball from the International Space Station (photos)
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a dramatic fireball from the International Space Station. The event was described as a stunning light show from above.

title: New this month, this Lego Star Wars Yoda bust would terrify Darth Vader and I have to have it
summary: Lego released a new Star Wars Yoda bust model, praised for its impressive design despite past issues with larger Yoda models. The product has generated significant interest among fans.

title: Spaceflight is hard on the heart, yet artificial ones grow better in space than on Earth
summary: Researchers found that mini-hearts grown from human stem cells develop significantly faster in space than on Earth, despite the negative effects of spaceflight on the human heart.

title: New for Star Wars Day, grab this Lego Star Wars model of The Mandalorian's coolest ship and get the Darksaber for free
summary: Lego released a new Star Wars UCS set featuring The Mandalorian's N-1 starfighter, and is offering a free Darksaber model with purchase for a limited time.

title: LAGEOS: An Earth Science Mission Built for Enduring Precision
summary: NASA launched LAGEOS, a disco ball-like spacecraft, on May 4, 1976, to study Earth's geodynamics using 426 retroreflectors. The mission remains operational, providing precise measurements of Earth's shape and movement.

title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center Director Janet Petro announced her retirement after a career spanning over 15 years with the agency, including roles as center director and acting administrator.

title: NASA Artemis II Crew Rings Nasdaq Closing Bell
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew rang the Nasdaq closing bell to celebrate the mission, highlighting international collaboration with CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen and NASA astronauts Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Reid Wiseman.

title: Key Support Equipment Arrives at Kennedy for Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA received key support equipment for the Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center's Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility. The HEPA wall modules will enhance clean room systems for launch processing.

title: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current
summary: NASA's STORIE mission aims to study Earth's ring current, a swarm of charged particles trapped by Earth's magnetic field that influences space weather. This research will help understand how Earth responds to changing space conditions.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA is offering a variety of STEM activities for summer, encouraging creativity, outdoor exploration, and future aspirations. The agency provides opportunities to engage with space-related learning and innovation.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. The galaxy is noted for its star clusters and striking spiral structure.

title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
summary: Heavy rains from Tropical Cyclone Maila caused a deadly landslide in Papua New Guinea's East New Britain region. The disaster highlights the region's vulnerability to extreme weather events.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington on May 4, with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and Irish officials in attendance.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission celebrated its 25th anniversary with a team reunion on April 15, 2026. A giant global map of Mars, created using Odyssey's THEMIS imagery, was unveiled for the occasion.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its Starship Flight 12 mission, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming flight aims to build on previous test efforts to advance the vehicle's development.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor who has experience in mass casualty events and overseas service discusses her upcoming mission to the ISS. The mission highlights the intersection of medical expertise and space exploration.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week, with launches planned from Florida, California, and French Guiana. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's Block 3 variant at the Cape, aiming for a launch later this year. The first flight of Block 3 is anticipated as the company continues testing and development.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a recent development in the space industry, though specific details were not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a significant event in space industry involving a parachute test. It highlights the challenges and importance of parachute deployment in space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for a Mars mission, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming it is on track for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The signal was captured at an optimal location, demonstrating the system's capability.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona observations. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force. If confirmed, he will become the third chief of space operations since the Space Force's establishment in 2019.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rover technology to support sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era designs. Modern lunar rovers are being developed to meet the demands of long-term surface operations.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which is seen as a significant step forward for the agency's future plans. NASA Administrator Isaacman describes the proposal as the agency's 'logical evolution.'

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion NASA budget, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. Lawmakers expressed disapproval of the administration's spending plan during the past week.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms raised €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in space industry startups. This trend highlights the expanding role of private capital in Europe's space sector.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit, leveraging microgravity to reformulate drugs for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has unveiled a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The tool aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with data on pre-existing conditions and risks in city skylines.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and multi-domain military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance York's market presence.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The orbital data center market is becoming competitive with major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space, along with satellite manufacturers such as Apex and K2 Space. The post highlights the growing interest and investment in orbital data infrastructure.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully hosted Liquid AI's large language model in orbit to analyze an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The demo, part of the Clustergate-2 mission launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite in March, marks a significant advancement in space-based AI processing.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, on a rideshare mission carrying a South Korean Earth Observation satellite. The mission highlights the growing role of commercial spaceflight and rideshare services in the space industry.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This collaboration marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiatives.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: The White House's nominee to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman prioritizes orbital warfare systems. Lt. Gen. Schiess Fielding emphasizes the need for more such systems.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense and military applications, as well as expanding network coverage in remote areas. The collaboration aims to leverage Sateliot's satellite IoT connectivity to enhance Telefónica's services.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discusses the company's focus on tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions for network resiliency at Modern Day Marine. The conversation highlights Lite Coms' product designs aimed at enhancing communication capabilities in various orbital environments.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management efficiency.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to enhance York's capabilities in satellite communications.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses a critical aspect of satellite communications regulation.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing for a crucial launch of six satellites this quarter to begin commercial operations. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming milestone in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force is launching an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.
